regime: Risk-Off Day 3 (Tech-Led Liquidation, Gold Safe-Haven Bid)
summary: The reversal became a trend. A soft June jobs print (+57K vs about +115K, May revised to +129K) sparked a dovish knee-jerk pop that reversed into a broad, tech-led de-risk. MNQ broke its opening range UP at 13:50 UTC (30,234) then FAILED, trending down all session to a 29,328.5 RTH low before a small bounce to 29,551.75 (-1.71%). It remains short and falling, 232 below its 29,783 stop. MES held far better: it printed a fresh record 7,593.75, reversed, and closed 7,522.75 (-0.14%), still below its stop, keeping the damage concentrated in mega-cap tech. The real tell was the hedge: gold rallied +2.26% to 4,164.7 on a clean lower-yields / safe-haven bid and flipped its 1hr stop back long, unlike Wednesday's whipsaw. Crude firmed +0.57% to 68.47 and flipped long. Equities are both-short on the 1hr, gold and crude now long, into a closed Friday and a three-day weekend. This is a third distribution day and a momentum unwind, not a broad crash, but it prints on a thin, holiday-shortened tape where a single data point can gap the weekend.

setups.name: TSMOM-20 1hr (FLAT, stopped out)
setups.entry: No position; the long is stopped out and the system is flat
setups.t1: Re-arm only on a 2-close reclaim of the 29,783 flipped stop
setups.bias: OUT_OF_HABITAT. Do not chase the downtrend short (long-only) or bottom-fish the -1.7% drop into a thin, post-jobs, pre-holiday tape. Wait for a clean 2-close stop reclaim (long).

setups.name: Gold Hedge / Cross-Asset Watch (MGC)
setups.entry: MGC flipped its 1hr stop long at 4,124 on a safe-haven bid (not a Net Alpha Pro trade signal)
setups.stop: A 2-close loss of 4,124 would void the hedge read
setups.t1: Overnight/RTH highs 4,181 to 4,185, then the 4,200 psych
setups.bias: A cross-asset tell, not a trade. Gold above 4,124 confirms the safe-haven bid supporting the risk-off read; a loss would signal the hedge fading. The long-only equity stack does not take it.

event.name: June jobs report (Nonfarm Payrolls) 8:30 ET
event.consensus: NFP about +115K (Dow Jones); May prior +129K after a downward revision.
event.actual: NFP +57K (a clear miss); May revised down to +129K; unemployment fell to 4.2% but on falling participation (61.5%, lowest since Mar 2021); leisure and hospitality -61K; average hourly earnings +0.3% MoM (+3.5% YoY).
event.surprise: Downside miss on payrolls with a misleading drop in the unemployment rate driven by people leaving the workforce. A cooling-labor signal the tape first read as dovish, then as a growth concern.
event.nqReaction: An initial dovish pop to about 30,320 pre-open reversed hard from the cash open; MNQ failed its opening-range up-break and sold off 1.71% led by mega-cap tech to close 29,551.75, below a falling short stop.
event.crossAsset: MES printed a fresh record 7,593.75 then reversed to 7,522.75 (-0.14%, outside day, still short). MGC +2.26% to 4,164.7, flipped its 1hr stop LONG on a safe-haven / lower-yields bid. MCL +0.57% to 68.47, flipped long. A genuine flight to gold, with the equity damage concentrated in tech.

observe.name: GAP_NQ (gap fade)
observe.note: A soft-jobs gap-and-reverse is not a clean gap-fade setup; watch Monday's post-weekend gap after a three-day break.

observe.name: TSMOM252LO_NQ (long-only 252)
observe.note: The longer-horizon long is intact above its multi-month base but tested by a third distribution day and the short-horizon flip short; watch closely.

observe.name: ES confirmation
observe.note: MES printed a fresh record 7,593.75 then reversed and stayed below its 1hr stop; the equity complex is both-short on the 1hr, keeping the damage tech-concentrated.

risk.throttleState: DEFENSIVE. TSMOM-20 is flat (stopped out, stop short and falling) and being long-only it stands aside. No in-habitat trend to size into.
risk.ddProximity: Not the binding issue with a flat book. The real risk is behavioral and gap-driven, not a gap to a stop.
risk.dailyLossBudget: Intact. The binding risk is chasing the breakdown short or bottom-fishing the -1.7% drop, then carrying it over a three-day weekend.
risk.sizing: No new directional risk without a defined in-habitat setup. Do not carry size into the Friday close; an overnight-and-weekend gap on a single jobs print is exactly what a flat book removes. The long-only equity stack takes neither the gold nor the crude long.
risk.note: Week ahead: Fri Jul 3 market CLOSED (Independence Day observed; Jul 4 Saturday); reopen Mon Jul 6 with ISM Services (Jun) 10:00 ET; FOMC June-meeting minutes Wed Jul 8 14:00 ET.

psychology: You do not owe the market a trade over a long weekend. Three red days and a soft jobs print create two opposite temptations: chase the breakdown short because the trend is now down, or catch the falling knife because 29,328 is 'down enough.' Both are the same mistake, needing a position. The system is flat by design; the stop already did its job at 30,242 two sessions ago. Into a closed Friday and a three-day weekend, an overnight-and-weekend gap on a single data point is precisely the risk a flat book removes. The professional move is unglamorous: protect capital and composure, let Monday's open reprice the jobs number, and demand a clean in-habitat setup before risking anything. Monday will still be there.

fullReport.title: Market Regime Snapshot (6-Axis)
fullReport.html:
AxisStateConfidence
DirectionRISK-OFF, tech-led liquidation, DAY 3. MNQ -1.71% to 29,551.75, closed BELOW a falling-short 29,783 stop (-232). MES -0.14% to 7,522.75, printed a fresh record 7,593.75 then reversed (outside day), also below its stop. NQ led ES lower again. MGC +2.26% flipped its 1hr stop back LONG on a genuine safe-haven bid; MCL +0.57% flipped long. Equities both-short; gold and crude now long.HIGH
VolatilityElevated, trend-day expansion. MNQ ATR(14) 174 (up from 110). RTH range 30,293 to 29,328.5 (~965 pts), a wide one-directional down day.HIGH
SessionFailed opening-range break (event trap). MNQ OR (13:30 to 13:44 UTC) 30,007 to 30,188 broke UP at 13:50 (30,234) then FAILED and reversed hard below the OR low, trending down all session to a 29,328.5 low. MES showed the same failed up-break after its record high.HIGH
InventoryNet short / long liquidation. A post-jobs dovish pop trapped buyers near 30,320; sellers controlled from the cash open, no VWAP reclaim, close near the lows.MODERATE
EventEVENT DAY (POST). June jobs report 8:30 ET: NFP +57K vs ~115K cons (May revised to +129K), a clear miss; unemployment fell to 4.2% on falling participation (61.5%, lowest since Mar 2021). Cash early close 1:00 pm ET. Ahead: Fri Jul 3 CLOSED; reopen Mon Jul 6.HIGH
LiquidityThin, holiday. The cash equity market closed early at 1:00 pm ET; full close Friday. Reduced afternoon depth amplified the trend-down.MODERATE

Cross-asset: Risk-off with a real hedge this time. A soft payrolls print popped equities on a dovish read, then reversed into a tech-led de-risk. Gold rallied +2.26% (lower-yields / safe-haven bid) and flipped its 1hr stop back long, unlike Wednesday's whipsaw. ES printed a fresh record 7,593.75 then reversed but closed near flat (-0.14%), keeping the damage concentrated in mega-cap tech, a third distribution day and momentum unwind rather than a broad crash. Crude firmed +0.57% and flipped long, a mild bounce. Drift vs Jul 1: reversal to trend, volatility expanded (ATR 110 to 174), commodity read rotated from de-risk-across-assets to flight-to-gold.

fullReport.title: NY Opening Range / COMPASS (MNQ)
fullReport.html: NY Opening Range / COMPASS (MNQ). OR (13:30 to 13:44 UTC) 30,007 to 30,188, width 181. Break UP at 13:50 UTC (30,234 above the OR high) that FAILED: price reversed back through the OR low and trended down to a 29,328.5 RTH low, closing 29,551.75. MES showed the same failed up-break after a fresh record 7,593.75. The failed break on the jobs-report open was the COMPASS tell of an event-open trap, exactly the setup the filter flags on NFP mornings.

fullReport.title: Momentum Breakout (Regime Filter)
fullReport.html: Momentum Breakout (Regime Filter). Long invalid. Price lost AVWAP, printed lower highs, failed its opening-range up-break and trended down; the 1hr stop is short and falling and the regime filter is negative. Fresh long signals require a 2-close reclaim of the 29,783 stop, not a first touch. The E3v2 momentum-long edge is out-of-habitat in a one-directional trend-down day.

fullReport.title: AVWAP Deviation Band Reversal (MNQ)
fullReport.html: AVWAP Deviation Band Reversal (MNQ). Center 29,581, sigma 180. Bands: +1s 29,760, +2s 29,940, +3s 30,119; -1s 29,401, -2s 29,221, -3s 29,042. Price closed 29,551.75, just below AVWAP in the -1s zone; intraday it stretched to the 29,328.5 low (through the -1s toward the -2s 29,221) then recovered to the mean, so it is not stretched to a reversion trigger at the close. The wide sigma (180 vs Wednesday's 65) reflects the trend-day expansion. A long-side fade re-qualifies only on a -2s/-3s tag with an exhaustion bar and a center reclaim, half size. Bands are price-based this run (volume unavailable; Alphatrends indicator not loaded), disclaimed accordingly.

fullReport.title: Strategy Habitat Check
fullReport.html:
StrategyStatusRead
TSMOM-20 1hr (ACTIVE, long-only)OUT_OF_HABITATThe long is stopped out and the 1hr stop is short and falling (price 29,552 vs 29,783 stop, -232). There is no long trend to trade and, being long-only, the system does not take the fresh short. Stand down; wait for a 2-close stop reclaim rather than fighting the downside.

Out-of-habitat today (single summary line, not shown as rows): the AVWAP Deviation Band, AVWAP Bounce, AVWAP Hand-off, AVWAP Midrange, AVWAP Back-Into-Range, and E3v2 Momentum Long edges all sit out a one-directional trend-down day. None of the seven Net Alpha Pro core edges are in-habitat.

fullReport.title: Yesterday's Map: Marked (Jul 1 map, checked on the Jul 2 session)
fullReport.html:
#Map rowResultMeasured
1MNQ reclaims the 30,339 flipped ATR stop on a 2-close basisNOT TRIGGEREDNo hourly close reclaimed 30,339; the best close was 30,258 (13:00 UTC) and price trended down from the open. The fresh short signal stood.
2MNQ accepts (2 hourly closes) below the 30,000 psych and 30,009 RTH lowTRIGGERED, PLAYED OUTSeven straight hourly closes below 30,000 from 14:00 UTC (29,841, 29,676, 29,462, 29,339, 29,488, 29,539, 29,552); the RTH low 29,328.5 blew through the old 29,976 reference and extended ~650 pts. The air pocket opened as mapped.
3MNQ chops between the 30,000 psych and the 30,172 AVWAP into the 8:30 jobs printNOT TRIGGEREDNo contained chop: overnight hourly closes broke both above 30,172 (30,203, 30,207, 30,258) and below 30,000 (29,921, 29,891), then the session trended down rather than ranging.
4MES loses the 7,500 psych on hourly closesTRIGGERED, MIXEDMES lost 7,500 on one hourly close (7,482 at 17:00 UTC) and printed a 7,479.75 low as flagged, but reclaimed to close 7,522.75 (-0.14%); it did not join NQ's decline. Both indices remain below their 1hr stops (both-short), yet ES's resilience kept the move tech-concentrated.
5MGC reclaims 4,123 (flipped stop) while MCL holds below 69.23TRIGGERED, MIXEDBoth IF legs held: gold reclaimed 4,123 (closed 4,164.7, flipped long) and crude stayed below 69.23 all day. The gold-hedge thesis played out (a clean +2.26% safe-haven bid), but crude did not stay heavy, firming +0.57% and flipping its own stop long.

fullReport.title: Tomorrow's Map (for Monday, July 6, 2026)
fullReport.html:
Map for Monday, July 6, 2026 (markets closed Friday July 3). Filters Monday: Monday gates out BIR (skip Mon); HOF, MID, and BNC are eligible by weekday. The HOF dead zone 12:00 to 14:00 ET still applies, and MID only in the 9:30 to 10:30 ET window. Treat the post-holiday open as a gap-risk open after a three-day weekend and a single jobs print. Calendar: Fri Jul 3 market CLOSED (Independence Day observed; Jul 4 Saturday). Next session Mon Jul 6 with ISM Services (Jun) 10:00 ET; FOMC June-meeting minutes Wed Jul 8 14:00 ET. All levels from today's 1hr chart.

yesterdaysMapScore.note: Risk-off Day 3. Row 2 (accept below 30,000 + 30,009) TRIGGERED and PLAYED OUT: 7 straight closes below 30,000 and a 29,328.5 low, about 650 pts of air. Rows 4 and 5 TRIGGERED but MIXED: MES lost 7,500 on one close (7,482) and a 7,479.75 low then reclaimed (-0.14%, still both-short), and gold reclaimed 4,123 while crude held below 69.23, but the gold hedge played out (+2.26%) while crude firmed and flipped long. Rows 1 and 3 NOT TRIGGERED (no 30,339 reclaim, no contained chop).
